sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano are in poor form, having lost their last 4 matches and failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 8 games. Atlético Madrid, on the other hand, have shown mixed results with 2 wins, 2 losses, and a draw in their last 5 matches, but their attacking prowess was evident in a 5-0 win against Real Betis. This contrast in form heavily favors Atlético Madrid ahead of the clash.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Atlético Madrid dominate the head-to-head record, remaining unbeaten in their last 19 encounters against Rayo Vallecano, with 4 wins in the last 5 matchups. The most recent meeting ended in a thrilling 3-2 victory for Atlético, and their consistent scoring ability against Rayo (no clean sheet for Rayo in 16 games) suggests they’ll likely find the net again.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano struggle offensively, sitting 19th in goals scored (18) and 18th in the league table with 22 points, while their defense has conceded 30 goals. Atlético Madrid, 3rd with 45 points, have scored 38 goals and conceded just 18, showcasing a balanced and potent side. Rayo’s high corner count per game (5.86, 4th in league) could offer set-piece opportunities, but Atlético’s superior quality should prevail.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Threats
sections.text: For Rayo, Jorge de Frutos (7 goals) is a key attacking threat, but their overall finishing has been poor with a low big chance conversion rate (30%). Atlético’s Julián Alvarez (7 goals, 3 assists, standout in head-to-heads with 4 goals) and Alexander Sørloth (7 goals) pose significant danger, likely exploiting Rayo’s defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Atlético Madrid’s superior form, historical dominance, and stronger squad, they are clear favorites with Sofascore predicting a 55% chance of an away win. Rayo Vallecano’s struggles and lack of confidence make it difficult to see them overcoming a motivated Atlético side. Expect a match with goals, likely favoring the visitors.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Rayo Vallecano leads Atlético Madrid 2-0 at Estadio de Vallecas. The home side has been dominant, converting their chances with goals from Fran Pérez and Óscar Valentín in the latter stages of the half. Atlético Madrid, despite holding more possession, have struggled to create clear-cut opportunities and have been unable to breach Rayo’s defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Rayo Vallecano's attacking dominance
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano has showcased a clinical edge in the first half, capitalizing on their big chances created. Their attacking play has been efficient, with numerous entries into the final third and penalty area. The home team displayed strong aerial and duel-winning capabilities, which helped maintain pressure on Atlético's defense. Players like Fran Pérez and Óscar Valentín have been pivotal in their finishing, while contributions from Andrei Rațiu in creating chances have kept Rayo in control.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Atlético Madrid's struggles
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid has found it challenging to break through Rayo Vallecano's defensive structure. Despite having more possession and significant penalty area entries, they’ve failed to register a single shot on target. Their expected goals reflect a lack of potency in attack, and defensively, they’ve been vulnerable, especially in aerial duels and overall physical battles. The away side needs a significant tactical shift to turn this game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano started the half with consistent attacking spells, frequently pushing forward in the opening minutes. Momentum often shifted in their favor during key periods, particularly in the middle of the half, where they built sustained pressure. Atlético Madrid had moments of intent, especially around the midway point, with a series of attacking phases, but their efforts waned as Rayo regained control towards the end of the half, culminating in their two goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Rayo Vallecano, Fran Pérez has been a standout with his goal and constant threat in attack. Óscar Valentín, apart from scoring, has been solid in midfield, contributing defensively as well. On Atlético Madrid’s side, Alexander Sørloth will be key to unlocking Rayo’s defense in the second half, while Nahuel Molina could play a crucial role in providing width and creating opportunities from the flanks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given Rayo Vallecano’s first-half performance, they are likely to continue pressing their advantage, focusing on maintaining their defensive solidity while exploiting counter-attacking opportunities. Atlético Madrid, trailing by two goals, will almost certainly push forward aggressively, potentially leaving gaps at the back. Tactical substitutions or formation changes could be on the cards for Atlético to inject freshness into their attack. The second half may see a more open game as Atlético chase the deficit, but Rayo’s current form suggests they can hold firm.
